title: Texas Southern University Reverses Commencement Plans After Student Pushback
body: When my daughter graduated from TSU in 2024, eight family members drove up to Texas, four went to the graduation the other four stayed behind along with eight or so friends and had a watch party of the graduation on YouTube.  It was the most longest, tackiest graduation I've ever been to.  The speakers spoke too long with it being 900 graduetes.  A lot of the graduates kept getting up walking, going to the bathroom I assume.  Once they received their degree, instead of returning to their seat, they left, no conferring of their degrees. Two graduations and some discipline I think will definitely help.
author: @trastep1239

title: Texas Southern University Reverses Commencement Plans After Student Pushback
body: I'm glad they spoke up and won! Selecting who can come to a graduation is so often heart wrenching. Let the families celebrate together! Four tickets is ridiculous!!!

title: Worst Universities in Texas #university #college #texas #fyp
body: I did a football camp at UNT. While I was there I saw the students using a lot of early enrollment recourses. The only thing that I personally think makes it a “bad college” are the dorm rooms. Dear goodness gracious. If you are arrested your prison cell will bear a striking similar resemblance to a UNT dorm. The beds are shaky and outdated held together by a rusty weld and the prayers that you don’t fall through the bed frame and the mattress will give you scoliosis. And the floors and corners are so dirty that eating off of a toilet seat is more hygienic. And the rooms are HAUNTED. I was there one year with my homie and our dorm was connected with another that was not being used. And I swear at night I could hear footsteps and creaking in that room, no one was in that room while we were there however. UNT for the love of EVERYTHING get some better dorms. Your dorm showers were A*S! THE TOILET PAPER GAVE ME THE WORST BUTTCRACK RASH EVER AND THE WHOLE TIME DURING SCRIMMAGES AT THE CAMP I WAS AT I HAD THE REEKING PAIN IN MY BUTTCRACK AND IN MY BACK FROM THE CRAPPY MATTRESSES, DO NOT LIVE IN THE DORMS FOR THE LOVE OF EVERYTHING
author: @DiNOS1GNAL

title: WHY I LEFT TEXAS SOUTHERN UNIVERSITY | PROS/CONS | WHERE AM I GOING TO SCHOOL NEXT? | lydcenzo
body: You would do better at Howard, Morgan State , Hampton or Lincoln. Much more of an east coast vide. However young lady , one of the main reasons for going away to college is to grow, become independent, and get your degrees. That way , when you return to NY , you will be on an entire different level. The first year away from home is always an adjustment. You never know how much you’re changing and growing when you go away to school until you return home and see your friends doing the same things you were doing before you left. Wherever you transfer to, I would suggest not going back to the city, but perhaps to a DC ( many HBCU’s in the area) which has great subway systems and weekend access to NYC( I am a Howard Alum💙❤️) and an excellent academic reputation. Black people are there from all over the world. Don’t arrest your evolution by going back to your familiar! You have started a personal journey of self reliance, critical thinking and expansion that will change your life forever! Good luck!!!! Oh and Howard has hella West Indians! Subway is right outside Howard’s gate and can be on the Amtrak pulling into Penn Station heading to Eastern Parkway in no time. Keep these pearls of wisdom. I get such joy out of seeing young people do good for the culture. Best of LUCK, Dr. DAVID B. FOLEY
author: @davidfoley726

title: WHY I LEFT TEXAS SOUTHERN UNIVERSITY | PROS/CONS | WHERE AM I GOING TO SCHOOL NEXT? | lydcenzo
body: You’ll change your mind in about 10 years. I dated a girl from Brooklyn and everybody I met in nyc during that time (all people in their 30’s) were miserable there and wanted to move. Some did but most stayed because according to them they feared getting outside of their comfort zone of their burrow. Just look at how as soon as people get above 30 and get money they leave for Florida, Texas, Cali or Atlanta. All your favorite ny celebrities got outta there soon as they got money. It ain’t the place you gonna wanna raise kids in, and taking public transport in your forties and carrying your clothes to the laundromat will get old.

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body:
Over the course of 2 months of editing, I realized a lot of the video games, anime, movies, and soundtracks I listened to during that time started to influence my video style, especially in the music selection. Can’t remember the amount of times I fell asleep in my chair while searching for the right track, but it was a lot, haha.

A few of inspirations: Your Lie in April, Xenoblade Chronicles, RL Grime, The Bear, Chef (Jon Favreau), Lofi Girl, and Ocean’s Eleven.

Sometimes I’d finish editing for the night, start playing video games, then immediately rush back to editing because of a boss fight, a cutscene, or a musical theme that inspired me to change up a particular song for a scene.

sounds kinda weird, but man I love video games and anime
author: @alvinzhoufilms

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I just drove 1300 miles from Minnesota to texas specifically to eat at goldees and experience genuine texas bbq. I’ve never had anything nearly this good. These fine people don’t just smoke meat, they make art and they are absolute masters at what they do. I WILL be back again!
author: @Sound_And_Color

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: You can tell the reason they’re so successful isn’t necessarily because of the food, it’s because they love the food they cook. They take an insane amount of pride in it. They love the process and every step it takes to get there. And the most important is they love each other.
author: @bretwomble8357

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: In September 2022 hurricane Ian hit my town (Fort Myers, FL) and we evacuated to my aunts house a cpl hrs away. While we were there me n my Dad came across this documentary and we both really loved the story. My dad loves good bbq restaurants and has traveled to a lot of different ones all over our state . The next month October 2022 while our town was rebuilding, i got a new job with a $3000 sign-on bonus. I told my mom for Xmas, as one of my dads gifts, I'm using that bonus money to fly me n my dad to Goldees bbq in March for his birthday. One month later on Nov 22, 2022 my father died unexpectedly. It crushed me. But I still took the trip in March 2023 to honor his memory n celebrate his birthday. I brought one of my best friends who my dad really liked in his place. We got there at 6am and we were number 9 n 10 in line. Best bbq I've ever had!  It's painful to watch this video but it also reminds me of my friend who got to experience waiting in the cold for 5 hours for BBQ with me lol. This year, on the same day from last year, me and that same friend went to one of our local barbecue restaurants we like here and had lunch in his memory and in memory of the trip we took. I love n miss you Dad and I'm sorry you didn't get to experience the #1 BBQ in Texas. Thank you Goldees and Alvin for making this unforgettable documentary ❤️🙏🏻
author: @beau2395

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I like how she's comfortable enough to be straight up about wanting her own restaurant. This shows the owners don't have big enough egos to want to supress that desire and have confidence they can fill her role.

body: "a lot of people think what we do is easy" I promise you brother I don't think what you do is easy, mad respect
author: @Trick0ut

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I'm from Texas, spent over 23 years in the restaurant business, and that is honestly probably one of the best, truest, behind the scenes look at a restaurant I've ever seen, short of being there. I hope this lets people realize the kind of work and dedication that goes into owning and running not just a bbq place, but a restaurant in general. You live and breath it. If you think of getting into the restaurant biz to just make a buck, to start one and then sell it for a bundle, because it's an easy way to make money.... DON'T!!!! you will fail. It's a way of life, not just a "job" or an investment. Fantastic video. Fantastic BBQ.
author: @bendalton5221

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: As a BBQ lover, this film was so captivating. The cinematography was awesome. The guys at Goldee's seem like such a chill group, no egos at all. Great to see and hear their story.
author: @carguyeyedoc

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I don't know what it was but this actually made me emotional thinking about back in the day working in a restaurant. Looking back at it you don't remember the early mornings, feeling exhausted or the anxiety involved with a busy service. You remember the good times joking around, prepping food, listening to music, playing video games, taking a smoke break and having real conversations. I don't know exactly what it is but you captured something awesome in this video and it made me feel something for sure.
author: @TrustYourGutz

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas

body: They are actually angled at him, it looks like they allowed it to happen. Which is dope b/c at the end of the day everyone wins and there is absolutely nothing wrong with showing a prior director love as he will forever be a part of the program. The band community is a very tight knit community to begin with. No animosity, no shade, just musicians doing what they do for the short time they have to do it in that setting and that level. Some of those kids may go to his college ass grad assistants in the future & vice versa, developing that pipeline benefits everyone in the end.
